sources.notes: Choose operations transforming each a_i into b_i and count optimal operation sequences modulo 1000003.
Official editorial fixes the number of doublings, decomposes residual additions by binary bits, and counts orderings with multinomial coefficients.

annotation.summary: Minimize the number of global doubling and per-element addition operations to transform vectors a into b, and count optimal sequences.
annotation.constraints: Values are bounded enough for bit decomposition and factorial precomputation modulo 1000003.
annotation.core_idea: The optimal number of global doublings is the maximum feasible common k, i.e. the minimum floor log ratio b_i/a_i. After fixing k, each residual addition decomposes into powers of two according to bits below k plus a final bucket before all doublings. The number of operation sequences is the product of multinomial counts for each stage.
annotation.complexity: O(n log A + MOD) preprocessing for factorials.

solution_variants.name: binary-stage-multinomial
solution_variants.summary: Choose maximal common doubling count, decompose additions by bit stage, and count permutations with factorials and inverses.

tags.evidence: Counting optimal sequences reduces to multinomial coefficients for operations performed at each doubling stage.
tags.description: Counting arrangements of grouped objects using multinomial coefficients or factorial ratios.
tags.created_reason: The broad combinatorics tag does not identify multinomial-counting problems.

tags.evidence: Residual additions below the chosen doubling count are counted by popcount/bit decomposition.
The number of doublings is chosen greedily as the maximum feasible common value.
